mnemonics: CRUD Your Data: Create, Read, Update, Delete - the four core operations to remember for any MongoDB query.
keyPoints: MongoDB uses a JSON-like document structure for queries, making it intuitive for developers familiar with JavaScript.
The find() method is the primary way to query documents, using a query object to filter results.
Aggregation pipelines allow for complex data processing and transformation through a series of staged operations.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that field names in query filters are case-sensitive, leading to unexpected empty results.
Misusing the $match aggregation stage by placing it after stages like $group, which filters the already grouped data instead of the original documents.
Using update() operations without the $set operator, which will replace the entire document instead of updating only the specified fields.
